Riverside Nursing Centre

CCN: 235535 · Grand Haven, MI 49417 · Ottawa County

Overview

Address
415 Friant Street, Grand Haven, MI 49417
Phone
6168424120
Certified beds
34
Avg daily residents
31 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1993-08-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.42 3.35 3.94 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.86 0.59 0.99 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.50 0.74 0.57 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.06 2.02 2.38 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.36 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.21
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.51
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.70
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
40.0%
annual
RN turnover
50.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.19
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.86
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-04 26 14 12 220 2 330
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-10 8 8 1 56 1 56
Total weighted health score
261.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (52)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
50 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. F 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. E 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0841 Designate a physician to serve as medical director responsible for implementation of resident care policies and coordination of medical care in the facility. F 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. D 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. D 2025-07-11 2025-08-08
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. E 2024-12-04 2025-02-19
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2024-12-04 2025-02-19
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. E 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2024-12-04 2025-01-15
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-16
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. E 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. F 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2022-12-14 2023-01-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-12-14 2023-01-20

Deficiencies 0755 — description Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. 0755.description

Deficiencies 0755 — tag 0755 0755.tag
Deficiencies 0756 — correction_date 2024-02-16 0756.correction_date
Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity D 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2024-01-10 0756.survey_date

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description

Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description

Deficiencies 0809 — correction_date 2025-01-15 0809.correction_date
Deficiencies 0809 — description Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. 0809.description
